devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । निशम्य कौषारविणोपवर्णितां । हरेः कथां कारणसूकरात्मनः । पुनः स पप्रच्छ तमुद्यताञ्जलिः । न चातितृप्तो विदुरो धृतव्रतः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaniśamya kauṣāraviṇopavarṇitāṃhareḥ kathāṃ kāraṇasūkarātmanaḥpunaḥ sa papraccha tamudyatāñjaliḥna cātitṛpto viduro dhṛtavrataḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having listened to the story of Hari in his Boar- incarnation (for the purpose of the lift-up of the earth) as narrated by Maitreya, Vidura who had taken the vow (of hearing the episodes of Hari) was not much satisfied. Folding up his hands (in bowing), he asked again.

devanagari: विदुर उवाच । तेनैव तु मुनिश्रेष्ठ हरिणा यज्ञमूर्तिना । आदिदैत्यो हिरण्याक्षो हत इत्यनुशुश्रुम ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: vidura uvācatenaiva tu muniśreṣṭha hariṇā yajñamūrtināādidaityo hiraṇyākṣo hata ityanuśuśruma
translation:
Vidura said:
“Oh great sage, we have heard it reported that the first daitya (demon) Hiraṇyākṣa was killed by the same Hari who (in the boar-form) was the embodiment of sacrifice (Yajña).

devanagari: तस्य चोद्धरतः क्षौणीं स्वदंष्ट्राग्रेण लीलया । दैत्यराजस्य च ब्रह्मन् कस्माद् हेतोरभून्मृधः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya coddharataḥ kṣauṇīṃ svadaṃṣṭrāgreṇa līlayādaityarājasya ca brahman kasmād hetorabhūnmṛdhaḥ
translation: Oh Brahman, for what purpose was there a confrontation between the demon-king (Hiraṇyākṣa) and him (Hari) who was lifting the earth up with the tip of his tusk.”

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । साधु वीर त्वया पृष्टं अवतारकथां हरेः । यत्त्वं पृच्छसि मर्त्यानां मृत्युपाशविशातनीम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācasādhu vīra tvayā pṛṣṭaṃ avatārakathāṃ hareḥyattvaṃ pṛcchasi martyānāṃ mṛtyupāśaviśātanīm
translation:
Maitreya said:
Oh warrior (Vidura), yours is a good query as the story about Hari’s incarnation that you ask, cuts asunder the noose of death in the case of mortal beings.

devanagari: ययोत्तानपदः पुत्रो मुनिना गीतयार्भकः । मृत्योः कृत्वैव मूर्ध्न्यङ्घ्रिं आरुरोह हरेः पदम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: yayottānapadaḥ putro muninā gītayārbhakaḥmṛtyoḥ kṛtvaiva mūrdhnyaṅghriṃ āruroha hareḥ padam
translation: By (hearing) which (story) as sung by the sage (Nārada), the male child of Uttānapāda (Dhruva) set his foot on the head of the god of Death and ascended to the place of Hari.

devanagari: अथात्रापीतिहासोऽयं श्रुतो मे वर्णितः पुरा । ब्रह्मणा देवदेवेन देवानां अनुपृच्छताम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: athātrāpītihāso'yaṃ śruto me varṇitaḥ purābrahmaṇā devadevena devānāṃ anupṛcchatām
translation: Even in this case (of the fight with Hiraṇyākṣa) I have heard this account related in ancient times by Brahmadeva, the god of gods, to the gods who enquired him (about it).

devanagari: दितिर्दाक्षायणी क्षत्तः मारीचं कश्यपं पतिम् । अपत्यकामा चकमे सन्ध्यायां हृच्छयार्दिता ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: ditirdākṣāyaṇī kṣattaḥ mārīcaṃ kaśyapaṃ patimapatyakāmā cakame sandhyāyāṃ hṛcchayārditā
translation: Oh Vidura, Diti, the daughter of Dakṣa, was desirous of a child and with her heart overcome with passion on one evening, desired (the company of) her husband Kaśyapa, the son of Marīci, who was seated in meditation in his fire-worship hall after offering oblations of milk to the Supreme Man, the protector of sacrifices, in the evening.

devanagari: इष्ट्वाग्निजिह्वं पयसा पुरुषं यजुषां पतिम् । निम्लोचत्यर्क आसीनं अग्न्यगारे समाहितम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: iṣṭvāgnijihvaṃ payasā puruṣaṃ yajuṣāṃ patimnimlocatyarka āsīnaṃ agnyagāre samāhitam
translation: Oh Vidura, Diti, the daughter of Dakṣa, was desirous of a child and with her heart overcome with passion on one evening, desired (the company of) her husband Kaśyapa, the son of Marīci, who was seated in meditation in his fire-worship hall after offering oblations of milk to the Supreme Man, the protector of sacrifices, in the evening.

devanagari: दितिरुवाच । एष मां त्वत्कृते विद्वन् काम आत्तशरासनः । दुनोति दीनां विक्रम्य रम्भामिव मतङ्गजः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: ditiruvācaeṣa māṃ tvatkṛte vidvan kāma āttaśarāsanaḥdunoti dīnāṃ vikramya rambhāmiva mataṅgajaḥ
translation:
Diti said:
“Oh learned, this god of love with a bow in his hand torments me, a poor thing, for your sake just as an elephant attacking a plantain tree crushes it.

devanagari: तद्‍भवान् दह्यमानायां सपत्‍नीनां समृद्धिभिः । प्रजावतीनां भद्रं ते मय्यायुङ्क्तामनुग्रहम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: tad‍bhavān dahyamānāyāṃ sapat‍nīnāṃ samṛddhibhiḥprajāvatīnāṃ bhadraṃ te mayyāyuṅktāmanugraham
translation: May you be prosperous! Please confer your favour on me who am burning at the (sight of the) prosperity of my co-wives who are blest with children.

devanagari: भर्तर्याप्तोरुमानानां लोकानाविशते यशः । पतिर्भवद्विधो यासां प्रजया ननु जायते ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: bhartaryāptorumānānāṃ lokānāviśate yaśaḥpatirbhavadvidho yāsāṃ prajayā nanu jāyate
translation: The fame of women who are greatly respected by husbands, spreads over the world, and of those to whom the husband like you, is really born as a son.

devanagari: पुरा पिता नो भगवान् दक्षो दुहितृवत्सलः । कं वृणीत वरं वत्सा इत्यपृच्छत नः पृथक् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: purā pitā no bhagavān dakṣo duhitṛvatsalaḥkaṃ vṛṇīta varaṃ vatsā ityapṛcchata naḥ pṛthak
translation: Formerly our father, venerableDakṣa, who was affectionate to us his daughters, asked us separately, “Oh daughters, whom do you select as your husband?”

devanagari: स विदित्वात्मजानां नो भावं सन्तानभावनः । त्रयोदशाददात्तासां यास्ते शीलमनुव्रताः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: sa viditvātmajānāṃ no bhāvaṃ santānabhāvanaḥtrayodaśādadāttāsāṃ yāste śīlamanuvratāḥ
translation: Having understood the inclination of us, his daughters, he, the progenitor of the race, gave you thirteen daughters who liked your nature.

devanagari: अथ मे कुरु कल्याण कामं कञ्जविलोचन । आर्तोपसर्पणं भूमन् अमोघं हि महीयसि ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: atha me kuru kalyāṇa kāmaṃ kañjavilocanaārtopasarpaṇaṃ bhūman amoghaṃ hi mahīyasi
translation: Oh lotus-eyed auspicious sage, now satisfy my desire. Oh plentiful one, an entreaty of the distressed to the great ones is never fruitless.”

devanagari: इति तां वीर मारीचः कृपणां बहुभाषिणीम् । प्रत्याहानुनयन् वाचा प्रवृद्धानङ्गकश्मलाम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: iti tāṃ vīra mārīcaḥ kṛpaṇāṃ bahubhāṣiṇīmpratyāhānunayan vācā pravṛddhānaṅgakaśmalām
translation: Oh valiant one, Kaśyapa replied in consolatory words to her who, being over-whelmed with passion, has become pitiable and was persuading him with many arguments (words).

devanagari: एष तेऽहं विधास्यामि प्रियं भीरु यदिच्छसि । तस्याः कामं न कः कुर्यात् सिद्धिस्त्रैवर्गिकी यतः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣa te'haṃ vidhāsyāmi priyaṃ bhīru yadicchasitasyāḥ kāmaṃ na kaḥ kuryāt siddhistraivargikī yataḥ
translation: “Oh timid one, here I am ready to comply with whatever you desire. Who would not fulfil the desire of her who helps accomplishment of the three puruṣārthas (goals) in life?

devanagari: सर्वाश्रमानुपादाय स्वाश्रमेण कलत्रवान् । व्यसनार्णवमत्येति जलयानैर्यथार्णवम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvāśramānupādāya svāśrameṇa kalatravānvyasanārṇavamatyeti jalayānairyathārṇavam
translation: Just as the people cross the sea in ships, a married person giving shelter to persons of other āśramas, crosses the sea of misery.

devanagari: यामाहुरात्मनो ह्यर्धं श्रेयस्कामस्य मानिनि । यस्यां स्वधुरमध्यस्य पुमांश्चरति विज्वरः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: yāmāhurātmano hyardhaṃ śreyaskāmasya māniniyasyāṃ svadhuramadhyasya pumāṃścarati vijvaraḥ
translation: Oh proud lady, they (the learned ones) call her (the wife) as the equal partner (lit. half the part) of the man who aspires after happiness. After entrusting the responsibility of his work to her, man can move about free from the fever of anxiety.

devanagari: यामाश्रित्येन्द्रियारातीन् दुर्जयानितराश्रमैः । वयं जयेम हेलाभिः दस्यून् दुर्गपतिर्यथा ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: yāmāśrityendriyārātīn durjayānitarāśramaiḥvayaṃ jayema helābhiḥ dasyūn durgapatiryathā
translation: Depending on whom (the wife), we easily overcome the enemies in the form of senses which are difficult to be conquered by men of other (non-gṛhastha) āśramas, just as the lord of a fort (subdues) the enemies (due to his fortified position).

devanagari: न वयं प्रभवस्तां त्वां अनुकर्तुं गृहेश्वरि । अप्यायुषा वा कार्त्स्न्येन ये चान्ये गुणगृध्नवः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: na vayaṃ prabhavastāṃ tvāṃ anukartuṃ gṛheśvariapyāyuṣā vā kārtsnyena ye cānye guṇagṛdhnavaḥ
translation: Oh mistress of the house, it is not possible for us to become so completely (obliging) like you, even (if we try) throughout our life (and in the next birth as well). Nor others who appreciate (your) virtues (can do so).

devanagari: अथापि काममेतं ते प्रजात्यै करवाण्यलम् । यथा मां नातिरोचन्ति मुहूर्तं प्रतिपालय ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: athāpi kāmametaṃ te prajātyai karavāṇyalamyathā māṃ nātirocanti muhūrtaṃ pratipālaya
translation: I shall, however, try to comply with your request (desire) for offspring. But please wait for a muhūrta so that they (the people) will not reproach me.

devanagari: एषा घोरतमा वेला घोराणां घोरदर्शना । चरन्ति यस्यां भूतानि भूतेशानुचराणि ह ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣā ghoratamā velā ghorāṇāṃ ghoradarśanācaranti yasyāṃ bhūtāni bhūteśānucarāṇi ha
translation: This is the most terrible of times when everything looks awful, as at this time goblins, the followers of Rudra wander about.

devanagari: एतस्यां साध्वि सन्ध्यायां भगवान् भूतभावनः । परीतो भूतपर्षद्भिः वृषेणाटति भूतराट् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: etasyāṃ sādhvi sandhyāyāṃ bhagavān bhūtabhāvanaḥparīto bhūtaparṣadbhiḥ vṛṣeṇāṭati bhūtarāṭ
translation: Oh pious lady it is in the evening that Lord Śiva, the creator of beings and the king of ghosts, moves about riding his bull and surrounded by his goblin-attendants.

devanagari: श्मशानचक्रानिलधूलिधूम्र । विकीर्णविद्योतजटाकलापः । भस्मावगुण्ठामलरुक्मदेहो । देवस्त्रिभिः पश्यति देवरस्ते ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: śmaśānacakrāniladhūlidhūmravikīrṇavidyotajaṭākalāpaḥbhasmāvaguṇṭhāmalarukmadehodevastribhiḥ paśyati devaraste
translation: Your brother-in-law[1] god Rudra whose shining mass of hair (on head) became scattered and tawny by the dust raised by the whirl-wind in śmaśāna (the crematory ground) and whose pure gold-like body is smeared with ashes, witnesses (everything) with his three eyes (viz. the Sun, the Moon and the fire.) Hence there is no secrecy anywhere.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Kaśyapa’s wife Diti and Śiva’s wife Satī were the daughters of Dakṣa. Hence Kaśyapa and Śiva were brothers-in-law, and Śiva was also Diti’s brother-in-law.

devanagari: न यस्य लोके स्वजनः परो वा । नात्यादृतो नोत कश्चिद्विगर्ह्यः । वयं व्रतैर्यत् चरणापविद्धां । आशास्महेऽजां बत भुक्तभोगाम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: na yasya loke svajanaḥ paro vānātyādṛto nota kaścidvigarhyaḥvayaṃ vratairyat caraṇāpaviddhāṃāśāsmahe'jāṃ bata bhuktabhogām
translation: In this world there is no one who is his relative or non-relative. Nobody is specially respectable or censurable to him. By performance of various vows, we desire to have the (prosperity and greatness of) Māyā which he has enjoyed and spurned (lit. kicked).

devanagari: यस्यानवद्याचरितं मनीषिणो । गृणन्त्यविद्यापटलं बिभित्सवः । निरस्तसाम्यातिशयोऽपि यत्स्वयं । पिशाचचर्यामचरद्‍गतिः सताम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: yasyānavadyācaritaṃ manīṣiṇogṛṇantyavidyāpaṭalaṃ bibhitsavaḥnirastasāmyātiśayo'pi yatsvayaṃpiśācacaryāmacarad‍gatiḥ satām
translation: Thoughtful people who wish to tear off the veil of Māyā[2] praise his life which is spotless or unattached (to sensual pleasures). He has none equal or superior to him. He is the ultimate goal of the saintly people; yet he himself followed the vow of piśāca type of life.[3]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
avidyā paṭala: nescience, the film or coating over the eye (of knowledge)—Padaratnāvalī, Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa

Footnote [3]:
Those who have realized the Brahman behave like an innocent child (bāla), a mad person (unmatta) or like a goblin piśāca. God Śiva adopted the third type of life. Or ‘lives in the company of goblins (piśācas)’—Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa Vj’s interpretation, “Under the order of Hari who has neither equal nor superior, Rudra followed the piśāca type of life” is far-fetched as we have to supply worḍs: yaś ca hariḥ...... tasya harer ājñayā, to the text of this verse.

devanagari: हसन्ति यस्याचरितं हि दुर्भगाः । स्वात्मन् रतस्याविदुषः समीहितम् । यैर्वस्त्रमाल्याभरणानुलेपनैः । श्वभोजनं स्वात्मतयोपलालितम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: hasanti yasyācaritaṃ hi durbhagāḥsvātman ratasyāviduṣaḥ samīhitamyairvastramālyābharaṇānulepanaiḥśvabhojanaṃ svātmatayopalālitam
translation: The unfortunate sinners decorate with garments, garlands, ornaments and pigments their body, (which is nothing but) the food of dogs, as if it (the body) were their Soul. They do not understand the motive behind his (Rudra’s) behaviour and laugh at him who is absorbed in (the meditation of) the Soul[4].
purport:
Footnote [4]:
VC. construes differently: without understanding the purpose of Rudra who is absorbed in Hari, that the garments etc. are to be offered to Lord Hari and hence I do not enjoy them.

devanagari: ब्रह्मादयो यत्कृतसेतुपाला । यत्कारणं विश्वमिदं च माया । आज्ञाकरी यस्य पिशाचचर्या । अहो विभूम्नश्चरितं विडम्बनम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmādayo yatkṛtasetupālāyatkāraṇaṃ viśvamidaṃ ca māyāājñākarī yasya piśācacaryāaho vibhūmnaścaritaṃ viḍambanam
translation: God Brahmā and others obey the limits (laws) set by him. He is the cause of this universe. Māyā is his obedient servant. His behaviour is like a Piśāca. Oh (how) incomprehensible are the ways of the Almighty[5].
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Padaratnāvalī interprets the verse as the glorification of Hari and not Rudra. Thus Rudra’s behaviour as piśāca is in obedience to Hari’s command. Incomprehensible are the ways of Hari the Perfect Being who makes gods like Rudra, the objects of worship by the world, obey him.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । सैवं संविदिते भर्त्रा मन्मथोन् मथितेन्द्रिया । जग्राह वासो ब्रह्मर्षेः वृषलीव गतत्रपा ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācasaivaṃ saṃvidite bhartrā manmathon mathitendriyājagrāha vāso brahmarṣeḥ vṛṣalīva gatatrapā
translation:
Maitreya said:
Though she was thus admonished by the husband, she whose senses were thoroughly overwhelmed with passion, caught hold of the garment of the Brāhmaṇa Sage like a shameless prostitute.

devanagari: स विदित्वाथ भार्यायाः तं निर्बन्धं विकर्मणि । नत्वा दिष्टाय रहसि तयाथोपविवेश हि ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: sa viditvātha bhāryāyāḥ taṃ nirbandhaṃ vikarmaṇinatvā diṣṭāya rahasi tayāthopaviveśa hi
translation: Knowing his wife’s importunity to that prohibited act, he then bowed down to the dictates (of the providence) and sat down with her in a secluded place.

devanagari: अथोपस्पृश्य सलिलं प्राणानायम्य वाग्यतः । ध्यायन् जजाप विरजं ब्रह्म ज्योतिः सनातनम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: athopaspṛśya salilaṃ prāṇānāyamya vāgyataḥdhyāyan jajāpa virajaṃ brahma jyotiḥ sanātanam
translation: He then took bath, controlled his breath and meditating silently over the pure, eternal Light, repeated internally the Gāyatrī (or the syllable Om).

devanagari: दितिस्तु व्रीडिता तेन कर्मावद्येन भारत । उपसङ्गम्य विप्रर्षिं अधोमुख्यभ्यभाषत ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: ditistu vrīḍitā tena karmāvadyena bhārataupasaṅgamya viprarṣiṃ adhomukhyabhyabhāṣata
translation: Oh Vidura, even Diti, feeling ashamed of that sinful act, approached the Brāhmaṇa-Sage and with her face cast down, addressed him.

devanagari: दितिरुवाच । न मे गर्भमिमं ब्रह्मन् भूतानां ऋषभोऽवधीत् । रुद्रः पतिर्हि भूतानां यस्याकरवमंहसम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: ditiruvācana me garbhamimaṃ brahman bhūtānāṃ ṛṣabho'vadhītrudraḥ patirhi bhūtānāṃ yasyākaravamaṃhasam
translation:
Diti said:
‘Oh Brahman! May not the Lord of goblins (bhūtas) kill the child in my womb. I have committed a fault against Rudra, the Lord of bhūtas (beings).

devanagari: नमो रुद्राय महते देवायोग्राय मीढुषे । शिवाय न्यस्तदण्डाय धृतदण्डाय मन्यवे ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: namo rudrāya mahate devāyogrāya mīḍhuṣeśivāya nyastadaṇḍāya dhṛtadaṇḍāya manyave
translation: Obeisance to the great god Śiva who is the remover of miseries (Rudra), who is irresistible, giver of blessings (to those who cherish desire and the highest bliss to the desireless devotees). He, though weaponless, takes up arms against the wicked, and is anger incarnate (at the time of destruction or pralaya).

devanagari: स नः प्रसीदतां भामो भगवानुर्वनुग्रहः । व्याधस्याप्यनुकम्प्यानां स्त्रीणां देवः सतीपतिः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa naḥ prasīdatāṃ bhāmo bhagavānurvanugrahaḥvyādhasyāpyanukampyānāṃ strīṇāṃ devaḥ satīpatiḥ
translation: May the Lord of Satī, my sister’s husband, the great god with abundance of grace, be gracious unto us women who are treated as worthy of compassion even by ruthless hunters.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । स्वसर्गस्याशिषं लोक्यां आशासानां प्रवेपतीम् । निवृत्तसन्ध्यानियमो भार्यामाह प्रजापतिः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācasvasargasyāśiṣaṃ lokyāṃ āśāsānāṃ pravepatīmnivṛttasandhyāniyamo bhāryāmāha prajāpatiḥ
translation:
Maitreya said:
Prajāpati (Kaśyapa) who has completed his evening rituals spoke to his wife who was trembling (greatly with fear) and who was desirous of securing the good of her progeny both here and hereafter.

devanagari: कश्यप उवाच । अप्रायत्यादात्मनस्ते दोषान्मौहूर्तिकादुत । मन्निदेशातिचारेण देवानां चातिहेलनात् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: kaśyapa uvācaaprāyatyādātmanaste doṣānmauhūrtikādutamannideśāticāreṇa devānāṃ cātihelanāt
translation:
Kaśyapa said:
On account of your impure mind, inauspiciousness of the (evening) time, disobedience to my order and complete disrespect for gods (attending Śiva), Oh evil woman, you will beget two wicked sons the worst ever born from wombs. Oh wrathful woman, they will frequently make the worlds and the protectors of the worlds, cry out (by their atrocious acts).

devanagari: भविष्यतस्तवाभद्रौ अभद्रे जाठराधमौ । लोकान्सपालांस्त्रींश्चण्डि मुहुराक्रन्दयिष्यतः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: bhaviṣyatastavābhadrau abhadre jāṭharādhamaulokānsapālāṃstrīṃścaṇḍi muhurākrandayiṣyataḥ
translation:
Kaśyapa said:
On account of your impure mind, inauspiciousness of the (evening) time, disobedience to my order and complete disrespect for gods (attending Śiva), Oh evil woman, you will beget two wicked sons the worst ever born from wombs. Oh wrathful woman, they will frequently make the worlds and the protectors of the worlds, cry out (by their atrocious acts).

devanagari: प्राणिनां हन्यमानानां दीनानां अकृतागसाम् । स्त्रीणां निगृह्यमाणानां कोपितेषु महात्मसु ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: prāṇināṃ hanyamānānāṃ dīnānāṃ akṛtāgasāmstrīṇāṃ nigṛhyamāṇānāṃ kopiteṣu mahātmasu
translation: When the poor and innocent beings will be massacred and women are seized per force and the great souleḍ ones are incensed, the venerable Lord of the universe, the creator of the world, will be enraged. He will come down as an incarnation and will kill them as Indra (the wielder of the Vajra) destroyed the mountains.

devanagari: तदा विश्वेश्वरः क्रुद्धो भगवान् लोकभावनः । हनिष्यत्यवतीर्यासौ यथाद्रीन् शतपर्वधृक् ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: tadā viśveśvaraḥ kruddho bhagavān lokabhāvanaḥhaniṣyatyavatīryāsau yathādrīn śataparvadhṛk
translation: When the poor and innocent beings will be massacred and women are seized per force and the great souleḍ ones are incensed, the venerable Lord of the universe, the creator of the world, will be enraged. He will come down as an incarnation and will kill them as Indra (the wielder of the Vajra) destroyed the mountains.

devanagari: दितिरुवाच । वधं भगवता साक्षान् सुनाभोदारबाहुना । आशासे पुत्रयोर्मह्यं मा क्रुद्धाद्‍ब्राह्मणाद् विभो ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: ditiruvācavadhaṃ bhagavatā sākṣān sunābhodārabāhunāāśāse putrayormahyaṃ mā kruddhād‍brāhmaṇād vibho
translation:
Diti said:
Oh Lord, I desire that both of my sons should meet death directly at the hands of the glorious Lord whose noble arms appear splendid by the disc (sudarśana). May not they be a victim of Brāhmaṇa wrath.

devanagari: न ब्रह्मदण्डदग्धस्य न भूतभयदस्य च । नारकाश्चानुगृह्णन्ति यां यां योनिमसौ गतः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: na brahmadaṇḍadagdhasya na bhūtabhayadasya canārakāścānugṛhṇanti yāṃ yāṃ yonimasau gataḥ
translation: Not even the denizens of hell show favour to a person burnt by Brahmadaṇḍa (curse of a Brāhmaṇa) and the tormentor of beings, in whatever class of creatures he be born.

devanagari: कश्यप उवाच । कृतशोकानुतापेन सद्यः प्रत्यवमर्शनात् । भगवत्युरुमानाच्च भवे मय्यपि चादरात् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: kaśyapa uvācakṛtaśokānutāpena sadyaḥ pratyavamarśanātbhagavatyurumānācca bhave mayyapi cādarāt
translation:
Kaśyapa said:
On account of the sorrow and repentance for your misdeed, and your present consideration of what is proper and improper, and due to your great respect to Lord Viṣṇu and regard for Śiva and myself one son out of your son’s son will be respected by saintly persons. They will sing of his pure fame which would be comparable to the glory of the venerable Lord.

devanagari: पुत्रस्यैव च पुत्राणां भवितैकः सतां मतः । गास्यन्ति यद्यशः शुद्धं भगवद्यशसा समम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: putrasyaiva ca putrāṇāṃ bhavitaikaḥ satāṃ mataḥgāsyanti yadyaśaḥ śuddhaṃ bhagavadyaśasā samam
translation:
Kaśyapa said:
On account of the sorrow and repentance for your misdeed, and your present consideration of what is proper and improper, and due to your great respect to Lord Viṣṇu and regard for Śiva and myself one son out of your son’s son will be respected by saintly persons. They will sing of his pure fame which would be comparable to the glory of the venerable Lord.

devanagari: योगैर्हेमेव दुर्वर्णं भावयिष्यन्ति साधवः । निर्वैरादिभिरात्मानं यच्छीलमनुवर्तितुम् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: yogairhemeva durvarṇaṃ bhāvayiṣyanti sādhavaḥnirvairādibhirātmānaṃ yacchīlamanuvartitum
translation: In order to emulate his pious nature, good persons will purify their hearts (by imbibing virtues) like non-enmity (friendliness to all) and others, just as they purify gold of inferior carat by purificatory processes.

devanagari: यत्प्रसादादिदं विश्वं प्रसीदति यदात्मकम् । स स्वदृग्भगवान् यस्य तोष्यतेऽनन्यया दृशा ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: yatprasādādidaṃ viśvaṃ prasīdati yadātmakamsa svadṛgbhagavān yasya toṣyate'nanyayā dṛśā
translation: By his intellect (or devotion) not directed to anyone else except the Lord, the venerable self-perceiving Lord by whose grace this universe becomes propitious, and who is the indweller of the universe, will be pleased with him.

devanagari: स वै महाभागवतो महात्मा । महानुभावो महतां महिष्ठः । प्रवृद्धभक्त्या ह्यनुभाविताशये । निवेश्य वैकुण्ठमिमं विहास्यति ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vai mahābhāgavato mahātmāmahānubhāvo mahatāṃ mahiṣṭhaḥpravṛddhabhaktyā hyanubhāvitāśayeniveśya vaikuṇṭhamimaṃ vihāsyati
translation: Verily, he would be the greatest devotee of unlimited vision and of great prowess and the greatest of the great. For, by his deep devotion, he will establish Lord Viṣṇu in his heart, purified by devotion, and give up the ego about his body etc.

devanagari: अलम्पटः शीलधरो गुणाकरो । हृष्टः परर्ध्या व्यथितो दुःखितेषु । अभूतशत्रुर्जगतः शोकहर्ता । नैदाघिकं तापमिवोडुराजः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: alampaṭaḥ śīladharo guṇākarohṛṣṭaḥ parardhyā vyathito duḥkhiteṣuabhūtaśatrurjagataḥ śokahartānaidāghikaṃ tāpamivoḍurājaḥ
translation: He will be devoid of desire for worldly pleasures, of good nature, a mine of excellent qualities, delighted at the prosperity of others and pained at the sufferings of the afflicted. With no enemy in the world, he will relieve the sorrows of the world, just as the Moon removes the summer heat.

devanagari: अन्तर्बहिश्चामलमब्जनेत्रं । स्वपूरुषेच्छानुगृहीतरूपम् । पौत्रस्तव श्रीललनाललामं । द्रष्टा स्फुरत्कुण्डलमण्डिताननम् ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: antarbahiścāmalamabjanetraṃsvapūruṣecchānugṛhītarūpampautrastava śrīlalanālalāmaṃdraṣṭā sphuratkuṇḍalamaṇḍitānanam
translation: Your grandson will see both in his mind and outside before him, Hari of excellent virtues, of lotus-like eyes, who assumes the form according to the desire of his votaries, and who is a grace to his consort Lakṣmī and his face adorned with refulgent ear-rings.”

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । श्रुत्वा भागवतं पौत्रं अमोदत दितिर्भृशम् । पुत्रयोश्च वधं कृष्णाद् विदित्वाऽसीन् महामनाः ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācaśrutvā bhāgavataṃ pautraṃ amodata ditirbhṛśamputrayośca vadhaṃ kṛṣṇād viditvā'sīn mahāmanāḥ
translation:
Maitreya said:
Hearing that her grandson would be a votary of the Lord, Diti was greatly delighted. Knowing that both of her sons would meet death at the hands of Kṛṣṇa, her heart was elated.
[back to top]
